title: Cloning the Self for Mental Well-Being: A Framework for Designing Safe and Therapeutic Self-Clone Chatbots
one_sentence_summary: This study develops a design framework for self-clone chatbots to support mental well-being by investigating therapeutic affordances, ethical risks, and safety guardrails through expert and user interviews.
research_problem: Self-clone chatbots offer potential for self-exploration but lack established design guidelines, posing risks such as identity confusion, negative reinforcement, and blurred user agency.
method_summary: In-depth qualitative interviews conducted with 16 mental health professionals (providers and researchers) and 6 prospective end-users to identify design tensions and opportunities.
dataset_or_materials: Qualitative data from interviews with 16 mental health experts and 6 general users, focused on mild, non-clinical mental health symptoms.
main_findings: The development of a tripartite design framework for self-clone chatbots that prioritizes therapeutic grounding, specific design dimensions, and safety guardrails for responsible development.

section_map.heading: Cloning the Self for Mental Well-Being: A Framework for Designing Safe and Therapeutic Self-Clone Chatbots
